title: Running sudo podman build fails with error running container: error creating container for in Travis CI with registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34 image
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

Running `sudo podman build` fails with `error running container: error creating container for ...` in Travis CI.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Have Dockerfile.fedora-34 which says
```
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34
RUN dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False /usr/sbin/init
```
2. In .travis.yml, install podman 3.1.2-1 with
```
. /etc/os-release && echo "deb https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/ /" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.list && curl -L https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/Release.key | sudo apt-key add - && sudo apt-get update -y && sudo apt-get install -y podman containernetworking-plugins
```
and run
```
- sudo podman build -t systemd -f Dockerfile.fedora-34 .
```

**Describe the results you received:**

https://travis-ci.com/github/adelton/freeipa-container/jobs/501611053
```
STEP 2: RUN dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False /usr/sbin/init
error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False /usr/sbin/init]: sd-bus call: Connection reset by peer
: exit status 1
Error: error building at STEP "RUN dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False /usr/sbin/init": error while running runtime: exit status 1
The command "$docker build -t systemd -f Dockerfile.$dockerfile ." exited with 125.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

No failure, image built fine.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

I did not run it but it's podman 3.1.2-1 installed from https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04.

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

I did not run it but it's podman 3.1.2-1 installed from https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04.

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

I did not run it but it's podman 3.1.2-1 installed from https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04.

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

This is Travis CI Ubuntu 20.04 VM.

comments.body: This worked fine with Fedora 33-based container in the same Travis CI setup.

I don't see how this is a Podman bug unless you can specifically point to an issue.  In my mind this looks like something in Fedora 34 installation is attempting to communicate with systemd and is blocked, since systemd is not running within the container.  I would report this as an issue with systemd.

Well, the same problem does not happen with rootless podman in Travis CI, and it does not happen with podman on Fedora and in GitHub Actions.

I don't see the relationship to systemd. Except maybe ... sudo-ed podman on Travis CI is run in such a way (with so many permissions) that it actually sees something to make it believe that systemd could be reached?
This error is coming out of Buildah, not out of the command inside the container - Buildah is failing to connect to systemd. Reopening, moving to c/buildah.

Apparently I don't have permission to transfer an issue to Buildah...

@TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind PTAL

Why would Buildah be connecting to systemd?  It has no idea what is going on within the container, and does not forward requests from the container to the host, this would be a serious security issue.

error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False /usr/sbin/init]: sd-bus call: Connection reset by peer

```
# cat > /tmp/Containerfile
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34
RUN dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False /usr/sbin/init
# podman build /tmp/
STEP 1: FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 3d89e185f185 done  
Copying config 5f05951e20 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: RUN dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False /usr/sbin/init
Fedora 34 openh264 (From Cisco) - x86_64        2.1 kB/s | 2.5 kB     00:01    
Fedora Modular 34 - x86_64                      2.0 MB/s | 3.8 MB     00:01    
Fedora Modular 34 - x86_64 - Updates            792 kB/s | 756 kB     00:00    
Fedora 34 - x86_64 - Updates                    3.2 MB/s | 5.8 MB     00:01    
Fedora 34 - x86_64                              4.5 MB/s |  57 MB     00:12    
Dependencies resolved.
================================================================================
 Package                   Arch        Version               Repository    Size
================================================================================
...                                                                                         
  systemd-pam-248-2.fc34.x86_64                                                 
  systemd-rpm-macros-248-2.fc34.noarch                                          

Complete!
STEP 3: COMMIT
--> ec36029448b
ec36029448bebaf03380e479f32b63942eedd0c81e227ef938aa3dac1c84bb37
sh-5.1# rpm -q podman
podman-3.1.2-1.fc34.x86_64
```
This worked fine for me.

The error message is being passed along from the container runtime's "create" command.  Is it `runc` or `crun`?  Which version?
Looking at the travis log, https://travis-ci.com/github/adelton/freeipa-container/jobs/501611053#L412 is also worrisome.
That "Please select an image" is from a subsequent `sudo podman run` attempt to use the image which was not built in the previous step. So nothing to worry about here.
In a scripted environment, where there isn't someone around to make a selection, I wouldn't expect us to prompt for something like that.
It will only prompt if a TTY exists.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Yes, I've rerun https://travis-ci.com/github/adelton/freeipa-container/jobs/501611053 now and the output is still the same.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

```
$ $docker build -t systemd -f Dockerfile.$dockerfile .

STEP 1: FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34

Getting image source signatures

Copying blob 7679c09af385 [>----------------------------------] 1.7MiB / 67.9MiB

Copying config 3567369c67 [====================================] 1.3KiB / 1.3KiB

Writing manifest to image destination

Storing signatures

STEP 2: RUN dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False /usr/sbin/init

error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False /usr/sbin/init]: sd-bus call: Connection reset by peer

: exit status 1
```
The question is why is this failing,  I don't see this as a buildah issue,unless something is happening in the travis environment to cause this to fail.

Running this locally works fine, so we need more reproducer on why this fails in travis.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have had no futher input, closing, reopen if more input is available.
